message:
feat(cli): add `setup` subcommand for auto-detecting agents and writing MCP configs

Introduce `am setup run` and `am setup status` commands that detect
installed coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, Gemini, Cline,
Windsurf, OpenCode, etc.) and write properly-configured MCP server
stanzas into each agent's config file.

Key design decisions:
- Agent-agnostic logic lives in mcp-agent-mail-core/src/setup.rs so
  the server and tests can reuse it without depending on the CLI crate
- Token resolution: explicit --token flag > .env file > environment var
- Config merging: reads existing JSON configs and deep-merges the
  "mcpServers" key rather than overwriting the entire file
- Agent detection reuses the existing detect_installed_agents() infra
- Hook installation auto-skips when AGENT_MAIL_AGENT is unset to
  prevent generating malformed commands
- Adds getrandom dependency for token generation when no token exists
- Mode matrix harness updated: `setup --help` allowed in CLI mode,
  `setup` denied in MCP-only mode
- CLI help fixtures updated to include the new subcommand

Subcommands:
  am setup run    Auto-detect + configure (--dry-run, --agent, --json)
  am setup status Show detection results and config state

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -15,6 +15,7 @@ thiserror.workspace = true
 chrono.workspace = true
 regex.workspace = true
 dirs = "6"
+getrandom = "0.2"
 shellexpand = "3"
 sha1.workspace = true
 fs2.workspace = true

@@ -30,6 +30,7 @@ Commands:
                               handshake)
   contacts                    Contact request/approve/reject/policy lifecycle
   beads                       Query beads (issue tracker) status for the current project
+  setup                       Detect coding agents and configure MCP server connections
   help                        Print this message or the help of the given subcommand(s)
